methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $1.8B - $6.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Public), post-money $2.6B

data.narrative:
Alto Neuroscience is a public-stage biotech and health company founded by Amit Etkin. The target universe consists of 3 same-sector, same-stage public peers: Tempus AI ($6.0B), Freenome ($2.6B), and Recursion Pharmaceuticals ($1.8B), alongside 8 additional private comparables including Abridge and Ultima Genomics.

The peer-median method is the primary applicable triangulation approach. The median valuation across the 3 public biotech comparables is $2.6B, with a range spanning $1.8B to $6.0B. A public-comp anchoring approach using sector price-to-sales multiples (median 7.8x across 3 public tickers: RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) cannot be applied, as Alto's revenue is not disclosed. With only one applicable method, triangulation yields a narrower estimate: low of $2.2B, median of $2.6B, and high of $3.0B.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $2.2B to $3.0B, centered on $2.6B. Key uncertainties include the limited sample size of directly comparable public peers, which constrains confidence in the peer-median estimate. Additionally, the substantial valuation spread within the peer set ($1.8B to $6.0B) reflects material heterogeneity in how public markets price biotech companies at this stage. Absence of revenue data prevents revenue-multiple validation, leaving the estimate anchored solely to comparable company valuations.
